name: grace_window_period_no_repause
description: Warm day with planned window period 06:00–10:00 (windows_recommended=True, hvac_mode=off). Sensor opens at 07:00 during window — planned window gate suppresses pause. Grace might start; sensor opens again at 07:05 during window → no re-pause (within window). No pause fires at any point during the window period.
notes: Tests: planned window gate suppressing pause when sensor opens during window period.
_is_within_planned_window_period() requires classification.hvac_mode == 'off'; the warm-day
classification with windows_recommended uses hvac_mode='off'.
Contrast with non-window scenarios where sensor_open during warm day → nat vent or pause.
Issue #476: migrated to use_coordinator=true, skip_startup_coalesce=true. sensor_open dispatches
via fake_hass.states.async_set() through the real _async_door_window_changed listener, reaching
handle_door_window_open()'s _is_within_planned_window_period() gate for real.
The 07:00/07:05 assertions were 'no_action' in the legacy-simulator version. outcomes.py's
production_outcome_at() has no 'no_action' sentinel — it carries forward the most recent real
decision. Corrected to 'classification_applied' (carried forward from 05:31) — if the window gate
failed to suppress the pause, a real sensor_opened/paused decision would fire at 07:00, and this
assertion would correctly fail.
wake_time/briefing_time moved to 04:00/04:15 (outside this scenario's 05:30-08:00 span): the
coordinator's default wake_time=06:30/briefing_time=06:00 fall INSIDE this range, and once the
async_track_time_change timer-callback bug (below) was fixed, the real _async_morning_wakeup()
correctly started firing at 06:30 — legitimately changing the carried-forward outcome to
comfort_restored, unrelated noise for a scenario specifically about the window-period gate.
Migrating this scenario found a second real harness bug beyond the sensor_check_callback one:
async_track_time_change/async_track_time_interval callbacks (_async_send_briefing,
_async_morning_wakeup — both async def) were constructed as coroutines by
FakeScheduler's heap-fire step but never awaited, because the coroutine was returned by an
inner _fire_and_reschedule wrapper, one level below where advance_to() checked for it. Fixed in
fake_scheduler.py: both _schedule_daily and _schedule_interval's wrappers now return the inner
callback's result so advance_to()'s existing coroutine check can see and enqueue it.
This was a real, previously-silent bug — any scenario spanning briefing_time/wake_time/
sleep_time/midnight would have silently dropped that callback's entire effect.
Revert test: temporarily disabled _is_within_planned_window_period()'s check in
handle_door_window_open (automation.py) — both 07:00/07:05 assertions correctly failed (fell
through to nat-vent activation instead of no-op). Restored; git diff confirmed clean.

events.note: Early morning. Indoor 72F, outdoor 64F. Conditions favorable for planned window ventilation.
Warm-day classification with planned window 06:00–10:00. hvac_mode=off during window.
Window opens at 07:00, within planned window period (06:00–10:00). Window gate should suppress pause.
Second window opens at 07:05. Still within window period — no pause, no change.
Mid-morning. Ventilation working, indoor cooling slightly. Still within window period.

assertions.reason: Warm-day classification applied: hvac_mode=off, windows_recommended=True, window 06:00–10:00.

Sensor opens at 07:00, within planned window period 06:00–10:00. _is_within_planned_window_period() returns True (classification.hvac_mode='off', windows_recommended=True, 07:00 is in [06:00,10:00]) — no pause, no nat vent, so the outcome stays the carried-forward classification_applied from 05:31. Corrected from legacy 'no_action' — see notes.
Second sensor opens at 07:05, still within window period. Already suppressed — same carried-forward outcome.

verdict.summary: Planned window period gate suppresses pause when sensor opens during the scheduled window
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (off, windows_recommended) → no_action on sensor_open (07:00, within window) → no_action on second sensor_open (07:05)
verdict.expected_behavior: During planned window hours, sensor openings are expected and do not trigger pause or nat-vent logic
